hero.title: Check Your Relocation Documents
hero.subtitle: Find out which documents expats often prepare when moving to the Netherlands.
introBullets: See where to obtain missing documents

image.alt: Document readiness checker for moving to the Netherlands showing document categories

imageFallback.alt: Document readiness checker for moving to the Netherlands

explanatorySections.title: What this document checker helps you prepare
body: This checker helps you see which documents are commonly needed when moving to the Netherlands: identity documents, civil records, housing and employment documents, travel documents, and education documents where relevant.
You get a personalized checklist based on your situation and can expand each item to see why it matters, where to get it, and whether apostille or translation may be required.
bullets: Housing — proof of address, rental agreement

explanatorySections.title: Which documents often create delays
bullets: Birth certificate and marriage certificate — ordering and apostille can take weeks
Diploma copies and transcripts — certification may be required
Proof of address — needed for registration and banking; timing can be tight
Sponsorship or employer documents — coordination with employer or IND

explanatorySections.title: Apostille, legalization, and translation awareness
body: Some documents from your country of origin may need apostille or legalization to be accepted in the Netherlands. Others may need a certified translation if they are not in Dutch or English. This checker flags where that often applies; for full guidance see our guides on apostille, legalization, and translation.

explanatorySections.title: Why document preparation should start early
body: Replacement and certification times vary by country. Some civil records take weeks or months; apostille and translation add more lead time. Starting early reduces the risk of last-minute gaps that delay your move, registration, or first admin steps in the Netherlands.

introParagraphs: This document readiness checker helps expats understand which documents are commonly prepared before and during a move to the Netherlands. It gives a personalized checklist and a practical view of what may still be missing.
Use it as a planning tool for moving paperwork, identity records, work and housing support documents, and family-related documents. It is general guidance only and not legal advice.

disclosure: This checker is for planning and organization only. It does not replace legal, immigration, municipality, or professional advice.

infographic.alt: Infographic showing the main documents needed when moving to the Netherlands including identity, civil records, employment, housing, education and travel documents
